<!-- Generated by spec-lite | agent: plan_feature | date: 2026-06-13 -->

# Feature: Auth Flows Enhancement — OAuth2 (Client Credentials + Authorization Code/PKCE) & HMAC

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-FP-AUTH-001
**Source**: Plan Feature agent (self-contained — no plan file)

Expand the Auth Store so users can authenticate requests with three additional industry-standard schemes — **OAuth2 Client Credentials**, **OAuth2 Authorization Code (with PKCE, manual code/URL paste)**, and **HMAC signature** — configured end-to-end (UI → store slice → persistence → request-time application in the shared service layer). Simultaneously, clean up dead/misleading existing config and enforce a strict **no-assumed-defaults** policy in the core implementation: the user supplies every parameter that affects how credentials are computed or transmitted.

This lets users target modern APIs (machine-to-machine OAuth2, user-delegated OAuth2, and custom HMAC-signed APIs) without resorting to workarounds, while keeping the existing API Key / Basic / Digest / OAuth2 Refresh types intact and correct.

## 2. Technical Context

> Standing rules (coding standards, architecture, testing, logging, security) live in `.spec-lite/memory.md` — only **additions/overrides** are listed here.

### Approach

- **DRY OAuth2 via a shared base.** All four OAuth2 variants (existing `OAUTH2_REFRESH` + new `OAUTH2_CLIENT_CREDENTIALS` + `OAUTH2_AUTHORIZATION_CODE`) share token-endpoint mechanics. Introduce `OAuth2ServiceBase` (extends `AuthServiceBase`) that owns: building the token request (form-urlencoded), applying the chosen client-auth method, POSTing via `httpService`, parsing the token response, caching, and refresh. Each concrete service supplies only its grant-specific params.
- **Pure, platform-agnostic OAuth2/PKCE helpers in core.** New `packages/core/src/utils/oauth2.ts` holds pure builders — `generatePkcePair()`, `buildAuthorizationUrl()`, `buildTokenRequestBody()`, `parseAuthorizationResponse()` (extract `code`/`state` from a pasted code or full redirect URL), and `applyClientAuth()`. Because `@wave-client/shared` depends on `@wave-client/core` (never the reverse), both the shared services and the core wizard import these helpers — single source of truth for param construction.
- **Authorization Code = two phases.**
  1. *Acquire (interactive, in the wizard):* generate `code_verifier`/`code_challenge`, build the authorize URL, user opens it externally, authorizes, and pastes back the `code` (or full redirect URL). The wizard exchanges `code` + `code_verifier` for tokens via `adapter.http.executeRequest()` (routes through the platform `HttpService`, so proxy/cert settings apply), then persists `accessToken`/`refreshToken`/`tokenType`/`tokenExpiresAt` onto the auth record.
  2. *Apply (request time, in the shared service):* use the stored access token; if expired and a refresh token exists, refresh transparently using `OAuth2ServiceBase`.
- **HMAC signing is service-side only.** No interactive step. At request time, `HmacAuthService` renders the user's `signatureTemplate` against the live request (method/url/path/query/host/body + generated timestamp/nonce), computes `crypto.createHmac(algorithm, secretKey)`, encodes (hex/base64), and places the result in the user-named header or query param (with optional prefix). Generated timestamp/nonce are also emitted to optional dedicated headers so they are actually transmitted.
- **PKCE S256 in core via Web Crypto.** `crypto.subtle.digest('SHA-256', …)` is available in both the VS Code webview and the web app (the wizard already uses `crypto.randomUUID()`); `generatePkcePair()` is async.

### Spec-Specific Overrides

- **No-assumed-defaults override.** Memory does not mandate defaults, but this feature makes it explicit for auth: every OAuth2 type carries a **required** `clientAuthMethod` (`'basic'` = `client_secret_basic`, `'body'` = `client_secret_post`) with no preselected default. The synthetic `expires_in → 300` fallback in `OAuth2RefreshService` is **removed** (absent `expires_in` ⇒ no fabricated TTL; token is fetched per request rather than cached with an invented lifetime). `token_type` absent ⇒ `'Bearer'` is retained but documented inline as the **RFC 6750 spec value**, not an arbitrary default. PKCE method and HMAC algorithm/encoding selectors have **no** preselected value — the user must choose.
- **Backwards-compat not required** (per memory). Removed fields (`base64Encode`, manual Digest params, unused `OAuth2RefreshAuth.accessToken`/`tokenExpiresAt`) are dropped outright; existing `auth.json` entries migrate implicitly because readers ignore absent/unknown fields.
- **Persistence unchanged.** Secrets continue to ride the existing `writeJsonFileSecure`/`auth.json` path (encrypted at rest only when `WAVECLIENT_SECRET_KEY` is configured). Migrating secrets to `ISecretAdapter` is **explicitly out of scope** for this feature (tracked in TODO.md).

## 3. Data Model (Granular)

> No `.spec-lite/data_model.md` exists. Auth is a file-persisted (`auth.json`) array of discriminated-union records, mirrored in the Zustand `auths` slice. Types are defined in `packages/core/src/types/auth.ts` and re-exported through the store slice and the shared service `types.ts`.

### Entities & Attributes

**`AuthType` (enum)** — add three members; keep existing:
- `OAUTH2_CLIENT_CREDENTIALS = 'oauth2ClientCredentials'`
- `OAUTH2_AUTHORIZATION_CODE = 'oauth2AuthorizationCode'`
- `HMAC = 'hmac'`

**`BaseAuth`** — **remove** `base64Encode: boolean`. Remaining: `id`, `name`, `enabled`, `domainFilters: string[]`, `expiryDate?`.

**`DigestAuth`** — **remove** `realm`, `nonce`, `algorithm`, `qop`, `nc`, `cnonce`, `opaque` (all challenge-derived and ignored by the service). Reduce to `{ type, username, password }`.

**`OAuth2RefreshAuth`** — **remove** unused `accessToken?`, `tokenExpiresAt?`. **Add** required `clientAuthMethod: OAuth2ClientAuthMethod`. Final: `{ type, tokenUrl, clientId, clientSecret?, refreshToken, scope?, clientAuthMethod }`.

**`OAuth2ClientAuthMethod` (shared union type)** — `'basic' | 'body'`.

**`OAuth2ClientCredentialsAuth`** (new):
- `type` (`AuthType.OAUTH2_CLIENT_CREDENTIALS`)
- `tokenUrl` (string) — token endpoint. NOT NULL.
- `clientId` (string) — NOT NULL.
- `clientSecret` (string, secret) — NOT NULL.
- `scope` (string, optional) — space-separated scopes.
- `audience` (string, optional) — `audience`/resource param for providers that require it (e.g., Auth0).
- `clientAuthMethod` (`OAuth2ClientAuthMethod`) — NOT NULL (no default).

**`OAuth2AuthorizationCodeAuth`** (new):
- `type` (`AuthType.OAUTH2_AUTHORIZATION_CODE`)
- `authorizationUrl` (string) — authorize endpoint. NOT NULL.
- `tokenUrl` (string) — token endpoint. NOT NULL.
- `clientId` (string) — NOT NULL.
- `clientSecret` (string, secret, optional) — omit for public PKCE clients.
- `redirectUri` (string) — must match the value registered with the provider. NOT NULL.
- `scope` (string, optional).
- `codeChallengeMethod` (`'S256' | 'plain'`) — NOT NULL (no default; UI labels S256 as recommended but does not preselect).
- `clientAuthMethod` (`OAuth2ClientAuthMethod`) — NOT NULL.
- *Token state (populated by the wizard exchange; secret):* `accessToken?`, `refreshToken?`, `tokenType?`, `tokenExpiresAt?` (epoch ms).

**`HmacAuth`** (new):
- `type` (`AuthType.HMAC`)
- `algorithm` (`HmacHashAlgorithm` = `'sha256' | 'sha1' | 'sha512' | 'md5'`) — NOT NULL (no default).
- `secretKey` (string, secret) — NOT NULL.
- `keyId` (string, optional) — public identifier, if the scheme includes one.
- `signatureTemplate` (string) — the string-to-sign with placeholders. NOT NULL.
- `outputEncoding` (`'hex' | 'base64'`) — NOT NULL (no default).
- `sendIn` (`'header' | 'query'`) — NOT NULL.
- `targetName` (string) — header name or query-param name receiving the signature. NOT NULL.
- `prefix` (string, optional) — scheme label prepended to the signature value (e.g., `"HMAC "`).
- `timestampHeader` (string, optional) — header to carry the generated `{timestamp}` value.
- `nonceHeader` (string, optional) — header to carry the generated `{nonce}` value.

**Supported `signatureTemplate` placeholders** (rendered against the live request; env-var `{{...}}` placeholders also resolved first):
`{method}` (upper-case), `{url}` (full), `{path}`, `{query}` (raw query string, no leading `?`), `{host}`, `{body}` (raw request body as string), `{timestamp}` (Unix seconds), `{nonce}` (random hex string).

**`Auth` (union)** — add the three new interfaces.

### Relationships

- `Auth[]` is the persisted collection (`auth.json`) and the `auths` array in the Zustand store; 1:1 mirror. No cross-entity FKs.

### Indexes & Constraints

- `name` is unique across the collection (enforced in `createAuthSlice.addAuth`/`updateAuth`).
- `id` is a crypto-random unique key per record.
- Discriminated union on `type`; each `AuthType` maps to exactly one service in `AuthServiceFactory`.

## 4. Files

**Create:**
- `packages/core/src/utils/oauth2.ts` — pure PKCE + OAuth2 param/URL builders + redirect-response parser.
- `packages/shared/src/services/auth/OAuth2ServiceBase.ts` — shared token-endpoint base class.
- `packages/shared/src/services/auth/OAuth2ClientCredentialsService.ts` — client_credentials grant.
- `packages/shared/src/services/auth/OAuth2AuthorizationCodeService.ts` — applies stored token + refresh.
- `packages/shared/src/services/auth/HmacAuthService.ts` — HMAC signing.
- `packages/core/src/test/utils/oauth2.test.ts` — unit tests for core helpers.
- `packages/shared/src/test/services/auth/OAuth2ClientCredentialsService.test.ts`
- `packages/shared/src/test/services/auth/OAuth2AuthorizationCodeService.test.ts`
- `packages/shared/src/test/services/auth/HmacAuthService.test.ts`
- `packages/shared/src/test/services/auth/OAuth2RefreshService.test.ts` — added with the refactor (no-defaults coverage).

**Modify:**
- `packages/core/src/types/auth.ts` — enum members, remove dead fields, add new interfaces + shared union types, extend `Auth`.
- `packages/core/src/hooks/store/createAuthSlice.tsx` — re-export new types.
- `packages/core/src/components/common/AuthWizard.tsx` — remove dead fields; add wizard sections for the 3 new types; interactive Authorization Code acquisition UI.
- `packages/core/src/components/common/AuthStoreGrid.tsx` — icons + labels for the 3 new types.
- `packages/shared/src/services/auth/types.ts` — re-export new core types.
- `packages/shared/src/services/auth/OAuth2RefreshService.ts` — extend `OAuth2ServiceBase`; add `clientAuthMethod`; remove fabricated `expires_in` default.
- `packages/shared/src/services/auth/AuthServiceFactory.ts` — register the 3 new services.
- `packages/shared/src/services/auth/index.ts` — barrel exports for new services.
- Auth documentation (README / docs covering supported auth types) — document the 3 new types, the no-defaults policy, removed fields, and the HMAC placeholder set.

## 5. Dependencies

- **None new.** Uses existing `crypto` (Node `createHmac` in shared; Web Crypto `subtle.digest` in core), existing `httpService`/`adapter.http`, and the existing `Result`/`authOk`/`authErr` helpers. No new npm packages.

## 6. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Remove dead/misleading auth config

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - In `packages/core/src/types/auth.ts`: remove `base64Encode` from `BaseAuth`; reduce `DigestAuth` to `{ type, username, password }` (delete `realm`/`nonce`/`algorithm`/`qop`/`nc`/`cnonce`/`opaque`); remove `accessToken`/`tokenExpiresAt` from `OAuth2RefreshAuth`.
  - In `AuthWizard.tsx`: delete the "Base64 Encode Credentials" checkbox + its state; delete the Digest realm/nonce/algorithm/qop/nc/cnonce/opaque inputs + their state and the `base64Encode` field from the built auth objects.
  - In `createAuthSlice.tsx` re-exports and shared `types.ts`: nothing to add, just confirm types still compile after field removal.
  - Confirm `DigestAuthService` is unaffected (it never read those fields) and `BasicAuthService` is unaffected (base64 is intrinsic to Basic).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: Update/verify `AuthWizard` tests — saving a Digest auth produces `{ type, username, password, ...base }` with none of the removed keys; the base64 checkbox no longer renders; saving an API Key / Basic auth has no `base64Encode` key. Type-level: a Digest object with a removed key fails `tsc` (compile check is sufficient — no runtime test needed).
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: In auth docs, remove references to "Base64 Encode Credentials" and manual Digest parameters; note Digest is fully challenge-driven (only username/password are configured).
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/core build` + tests pass; opening the Add-Auth wizard for Digest shows only Username/Password; saved Digest record JSON contains no removed fields.

### TASK-002: OAuth2 shared base + core helpers; refactor existing Refresh (no defaults)

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Create `packages/core/src/utils/oauth2.ts` with pure functions: `generatePkcePair(method: 'S256' | 'plain'): Promise<{ codeVerifier: string; codeChallenge: string }>` (verifier = base64url of 32 random bytes; S256 challenge = base64url(SHA-256(verifier)) via `crypto.subtle`; plain challenge = verifier); `buildAuthorizationUrl(params)`; `buildTokenRequestBody(grantParams): URLSearchParams`; `applyClientAuth(method, clientId, clientSecret, body, headers)` (sets `Authorization: Basic base64(id:secret)` for `'basic'`, or appends `client_id`/`client_secret` to the body for `'body'`); `parseAuthorizationResponse(input: string): { code?: string; state?: string; error?: string }` (accepts a bare code or a full redirect URL).
  - Create `OAuth2ServiceBase` (extends `AuthServiceBase`): `protected async fetchToken(tokenUrl, grantParams, clientAuthMethod, clientId, clientSecret): Promise<OAuth2TokenResult>` using `httpService.send` with `application/x-www-form-urlencoded`; `protected parseTokenResponse(data)` → `{ accessToken, refreshToken?, tokenType, expiresAt? }` (no fabricated `expires_in`; `tokenType` falls back to `'Bearer'` per RFC 6750 with an inline comment); `protected cacheToken`/`getCachedToken` with the existing expiry-buffer logic, skipping caching when `expiresAt` is undefined.
  - Refactor `OAuth2RefreshService` to extend `OAuth2ServiceBase`: add required `clientAuthMethod` handling; build `grant_type=refresh_token` params via the base; remove the `|| 300` default and the inline body-building now owned by the base.
  - Add `clientAuthMethod` to `OAuth2RefreshAuth` (TASK-001 already touched this type) and surface it in the existing Refresh wizard section as a required Select (`basic`/`body`, no preselected default; validate it is chosen).
- [ ] **Unit Tests**:
  - `oauth2.test.ts`: PKCE S256 challenge equals known SHA-256/base64url vector; `plain` returns verifier unchanged; `applyClientAuth('basic', …)` sets the correct `Authorization` header and omits secret from body; `applyClientAuth('body', …)` does the inverse; `parseAuthorizationResponse` extracts `code`+`state` from a full redirect URL and from a bare code, and surfaces `error` from an error redirect.
  - `OAuth2RefreshService.test.ts`: refreshes and sets `Authorization: <tokenType> <accessToken>`; honors `clientAuthMethod` basic vs body (assert outbound request shape via mocked `httpService`); when the token response omits `expires_in`, the token is **not** cached with a synthetic TTL (a second call re-fetches); unresolved env placeholders return `authErr`.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Document `OAuth2ServiceBase` responsibilities and the `clientAuthMethod` semantics; note the removal of the `expires_in` default and the RFC-mandated `token_type` fallback.
- **Verify**: Shared tests pass; an existing OAuth2 Refresh auth still authenticates (header-based and body-based client auth both exercised in tests).
- **Depends on**: TASK-001

### TASK-003: OAuth2 Client Credentials (end-to-end)

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Types: add `AuthType.OAUTH2_CLIENT_CREDENTIALS`, `OAuth2ClientCredentialsAuth`, and `OAuth2ClientAuthMethod`; extend `Auth` union; re-export through slice + shared `types.ts`.
  - Service: create `OAuth2ClientCredentialsService` (extends `OAuth2ServiceBase`). `applyAuth` validates type/config, resolves env placeholders for `tokenUrl`/`clientId`/`clientSecret`/`scope`/`audience`, skips if `Authorization` already present, then `fetchToken` with `grant_type=client_credentials` (+ `scope`/`audience` when provided), caches by `auth.id`, returns `Authorization: <tokenType> <accessToken>`.
  - Factory: register the new `AuthType` → service; add to `index.ts` barrel.
  - Wizard: add a Client Credentials section — `Token URL*`, `Client ID*`, `Client Secret*` (masked, with show/hide), `Client Auth Method*` (Select basic/body, no default), `Scope`, `Audience`; add type to the Auth-Type Select; add validation for the four required fields.
  - Grid: add icon (`KeyRoundIcon` or similar from existing Lucide set) + label "OAuth2 Client Credentials".
- [ ] **Unit Tests**:
  - Service: obtains and applies a token; `clientAuthMethod` basic vs body produce the correct outbound request (mocked `httpService`); includes `scope`/`audience` only when set; missing required field surfaces `authErr`; cached token is reused within its lifetime.
  - Wizard: selecting Client Credentials renders the fields; submitting without a required field shows the error; a valid submit calls `onSave` with a correctly-shaped `OAuth2ClientCredentialsAuth`.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add an "OAuth2 Client Credentials" entry to the supported-auth-types docs (fields, when to use, no-defaults note).
- **Verify**: Add a Client Credentials auth in the UI, save, confirm it persists to `auth.json`, and a request to a matching domain receives a Bearer header in tests/manual run.
- **Depends on**: TASK-002

### TASK-004: OAuth2 Authorization Code — request-time service (apply stored token + refresh)

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Types: add `AuthType.OAUTH2_AUTHORIZATION_CODE` and `OAuth2AuthorizationCodeAuth` (with token-state fields); extend `Auth` union; re-export.
  - Service: create `OAuth2AuthorizationCodeService` (extends `OAuth2ServiceBase`). `applyAuth`: validate type/config; if a non-expired `accessToken` is present (check `tokenExpiresAt` with the expiry buffer), apply `Authorization: <tokenType> <accessToken>`; if expired/missing and a `refreshToken` exists, `fetchToken` with `grant_type=refresh_token` honoring `clientAuthMethod`, update the in-memory cache, and apply the new token; if no usable token and no refresh token, return `authErr('No access token — re-authorize this OAuth2 auth')`. (Note: persisting refreshed tokens back to `auth.json` is not required for correctness because the in-memory cache covers the session; document this.)
  - Factory + barrel: register service.
- [ ] **Unit Tests**: applies a valid stored token without calling the network; refreshes via `refreshToken` when expired and applies the new token; honors `clientAuthMethod`; returns the re-authorize error when no token and no refresh token exist; respects domain filters/expiry/enabled via `validateAuth`.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Document the Authorization Code request-time behavior (stored token → refresh → re-authorize) in auth docs.
- **Verify**: Shared tests pass; with a seeded `OAuth2AuthorizationCodeAuth` (mock tokens), a request applies/refreshes the Bearer header as expected.
- **Depends on**: TASK-002

### TASK-005: OAuth2 Authorization Code — wizard interactive acquisition (PKCE + manual paste)

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Wizard: add an Authorization Code section with fields `Authorization URL*`, `Token URL*`, `Client ID*`, `Client Secret` (optional, masked), `Redirect URI*`, `Scope`, `Code Challenge Method*` (Select S256/plain, no default; S256 labeled recommended), `Client Auth Method*` (Select basic/body).
  - Acquisition sub-flow (in the wizard, using `useAdapter().http`):
    1. **Generate & Open**: on click, call `generatePkcePair(method)`, build the authorize URL via `buildAuthorizationUrl()` (include `response_type=code`, `client_id`, `redirect_uri`, `scope?`, `state` (random), `code_challenge`, `code_challenge_method`), display the URL with an **Open** action (`window.open`/external) and a read-only copy of it; stash `codeVerifier` + `state` in component state.
    2. **Paste & Exchange**: provide an input for the pasted authorization response (bare code or full redirect URL); on **Exchange**, `parseAuthorizationResponse()` → validate returned `state` matches, surface `error` if present; build token-exchange params (`grant_type=authorization_code`, `code`, `redirect_uri`, `code_verifier`, client auth per `clientAuthMethod`) and POST via `adapter.http.executeRequest` (form-urlencoded body, `Content-Type` header); on success store `accessToken`/`refreshToken`/`tokenType`/`tokenExpiresAt` into form state and show a success indicator; on failure show the endpoint error.
  - `handleSave` writes the full `OAuth2AuthorizationCodeAuth` including any acquired token state. Validation: required config fields present; warn (non-blocking) if saving without having acquired a token.
  - Grid: add icon + label "OAuth2 Authorization Code".
- [ ] **Unit Tests** (core, with a mock adapter):
  - Renders the section and the Generate/Open + Paste/Exchange controls when the type is selected.
  - Generate produces an authorize URL containing `code_challenge`, `code_challenge_method`, `state`, and `redirect_uri`.
  - Exchange with a mocked `adapter.http.executeRequest` returning a token response stores the tokens and shows success; a state mismatch blocks the exchange; an error redirect surfaces the provider error.
  - `onSave` yields a correctly-shaped `OAuth2AuthorizationCodeAuth`.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add a step-by-step "OAuth2 Authorization Code (PKCE, manual paste)" guide: register redirect URI → Generate & Open → authorize → paste code/URL → Exchange → Save; note codes are single-use/short-lived (exchange immediately).
- **Verify**: In a real run, configure against a test provider, complete the manual exchange, save, and confirm a subsequent request carries the Bearer token.
- **Depends on**: TASK-004

### TASK-006: HMAC authentication (end-to-end)

- [ ] **Implementation**:
  - Types: add `AuthType.HMAC`, `HmacAuth`, and `HmacHashAlgorithm`; extend `Auth` union; re-export.
  - Service: create `HmacAuthService` (extends `AuthServiceBase`). `applyAuth`: validate type/config; resolve env placeholders in `secretKey`/`keyId`/`signatureTemplate`/`targetName`/`prefix`; generate `timestamp` (Unix seconds) and `nonce` (random hex) once; render `signatureTemplate` by substituting `{method}`/`{url}`/`{path}`/`{query}`/`{host}`/`{body}`/`{timestamp}`/`{nonce}` from `config`; compute `crypto.createHmac(algorithm, secretKey).update(rendered).digest(outputEncoding)`; prepend `prefix` if set; return `headers`/`queryParams` placing the signature under `targetName` per `sendIn`, plus `timestampHeader`/`nonceHeader` headers when configured. Respect existing-header non-overwrite semantics like `ApiKeyAuthService`.
  - Factory + barrel: register service.
  - Wizard: add an HMAC section — `Algorithm*` (Select sha256/sha1/sha512/md5, no default), `Secret Key*` (masked), `Key ID`, `Signature Template*` (textarea + an inline list of supported placeholders), `Output Encoding*` (Select hex/base64, no default), `Send In*` (header/query), `Target Name*`, `Prefix`, `Timestamp Header`, `Nonce Header`; validate the required fields.
  - Grid: add icon (e.g., `FingerprintIcon`) + label "HMAC".
- [ ] **Unit Tests**:
  - Service: known-vector HMAC for a fixed template/secret/algorithm in both hex and base64; placeholders resolve from the request (`{method}` upper-cased, `{path}`/`{query}`/`{host}` from the URL, `{body}` raw); signature placed in header vs query per `sendIn`; `prefix` prepended; `timestampHeader`/`nonceHeader` emitted only when set and consistent with the values substituted into the template; missing required field → `authErr`; unresolved env placeholder → `authErr`.
  - Wizard: HMAC fields render; required-field validation; valid submit shapes a correct `HmacAuth`.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add an "HMAC" section to auth docs listing every supported placeholder, the encoding/target options, and a worked example (e.g., sign `{method}\n{path}\n{timestamp}` into `X-Signature` with a `X-Timestamp` companion header).
- **Verify**: Configure an HMAC auth, save, confirm `auth.json` persistence, and confirm in tests/manual run that the request carries the computed signature (and companion timestamp/nonce) headers.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001

## 7. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: This feature *is* the auth subsystem. Every new type flows through `AuthServiceFactory.getService(type)` and is applied in `HttpService` exactly like existing types (headers/queryParams merge, or—not used here—internal handling). New types respect the existing `validateAuth` gate (enabled / not expired / domain match) and env-var placeholder resolution via `AuthServiceBase.resolveValue`.
- **Error Handling**: All service paths return `Result` via `authOk`/`authErr` — never throw across the boundary; token-endpoint/exchange failures surface the provider's `error_description`/`error` where available. Wizard acquisition errors are shown inline (Banner/field error), consistent with the existing wizard. Unresolved `{{env}}` placeholders short-circuit to `authErr` before any network call.
- **Logging**: Follow memory's rule — **never log secrets, tokens, passwords, HMAC keys, client secrets, or authorization codes**. Log only non-sensitive context (auth name/id, type, token-endpoint host, HTTP status) when diagnosing failures. Authorization codes and `code_verifier` must never be logged.
- **Security**: Secret fields are masked in the UI with show/hide toggles (matching existing fields). PKCE `state` is validated on the exchange to prevent CSRF/code-injection. `S256` is the recommended challenge method (labeled as such). Secrets persist via the existing `writeJsonFileSecure` path only; no new plaintext sinks. Secret-adapter migration is deferred (TODO).
